title: Planet Labs' Government Deals Strengthen Recurring Revenue Base
text: Planet Labs PBC has secured two significant government contracts that strengthen its recurring revenue base. The company signed a nine-figure multiyear contract with the Swedish Armed Forces alongside an enterprise agreement with Slovenia's government. These represent the company's third major government partnership within twelve months. These agreements support Planet Labs' strategic shift toward solution-based services rather than raw imagery sales. The Slovenian deal demonstrates how recurring contracts address agriculture, urban planning and disaster response across public administration. Combined with the existing $734 million backlog, these wins provide enhanced revenue visibility. The contracts indicate growing government trust in Planet Labs' satellite data for defense and civilian applications. This positions the company competitively against rivals like Maxar and Airbus in the earth observation market. Planet Labs remains unprofitable, with analysts forecasting no profitability within three years. Short interest stands at 13.45%. Success depends on translating contract wins into improved cash flow and sustained performance.
